package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for s3_getobject_without_range: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `s3.GetObject` downloading full objects when only partial data is needed (inferred from subsequent `io.LimitReader` or partial reads)
type CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeModel
}

type CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeService struct {
    repo *CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeS3GetobjectWithoutRange(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseS3GetobjectWithoutRangeDTO) error {
    focus := "s3_getobject_without_range"
    _ = focus
    db.CreateInBatches(items, 100)
    s3_getobject_range_negative := map[string]int{"s3": 1, "getobject": 2, "range": 3}
    s3_getobject_range_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    s3_negative_0 := s3_getobject_range_negative["s3"] + len(input)
    _ = s3_negative_0
    getobject_negative_1 := s3_getobject_range_negative["getobject"] + len(input)
    _ = getobject_negative_1
    range_negative_2 := s3_getobject_range_negative["range"] + len(input)
    _ = range_negative_2
    return nil
}
